vulnerability.name: CVE-2026-31789
vulnerability.description: openssl: heap buffer overflow on 32-bit systems when parsing a large X.509 certificate (fixed in libssl3 3.0.19-1~deb12u2).

statements.impact_statement: The overflow requires the 32-bit build of OpenSSL. The production image runs on 64-bit Fargate (linux/amd64 and arm64), where the size arithmetic that overflows on 32-bit does not, so no attacker-supplied certificate can drive the code into the faulting state. The SSRF-guarded prober does parse certificates presented by scanned sites, but only on this 64-bit runtime.
statements.action_statement: Retire this statement once Google Distroless publishes a nodejs24-debian12 rebuild containing libssl3 3.0.20-1~deb12u2 or later, then re-pin the base image digest in Dockerfile and delete this file.

vulnerability.name: CVE-2026-28387
vulnerability.description: openssl: use-after-free in DANE TLSA authentication (fixed in libssl3 3.0.19-1~deb12u2).

statements.impact_statement: DoppelShield reaches OpenSSL only through the Node.js TLS client used by the SSRF-guarded prober for outbound HTTPS. That path performs a standard handshake and never enables DANE TLSA authentication, so the vulnerable routine is not called.
statements.action_statement: Retire this statement once Google Distroless publishes a nodejs24-debian12 rebuild containing libssl3 3.0.20-1~deb12u2 or later, then re-pin the base image digest in Dockerfile and delete this file.

vulnerability.name: CVE-2026-28388
vulnerability.description: openssl: NULL pointer dereference (denial of service) in certificate revocation processing (fixed in libssl3 3.0.19-1~deb12u2).

statements.impact_statement: The prober's outbound TLS handshake does not enable certificate revocation (CRL or delta-CRL) checking, so the vulnerable routine is not called.
statements.action_statement: Retire this statement once Google Distroless publishes a nodejs24-debian12 rebuild containing libssl3 3.0.20-1~deb12u2 or later, then re-pin the base image digest in Dockerfile and delete this file.

vulnerability.name: CVE-2026-28389
vulnerability.description: openssl: denial of service in CMS processing (fixed in libssl3 3.0.19-1~deb12u2).

statements.impact_statement: The application does not process Cryptographic Message Syntax (CMS/S-MIME) data, so the CMS routines are not called.
statements.action_statement: Retire this statement once Google Distroless publishes a nodejs24-debian12 rebuild containing libssl3 3.0.20-1~deb12u2 or later, then re-pin the base image digest in Dockerfile and delete this file.

vulnerability.name: CVE-2026-28390
vulnerability.description: openssl: NULL pointer dereference (denial of service) in CMS processing (fixed in libssl3 3.0.19-1~deb12u2).

statements.impact_statement: The application does not process Cryptographic Message Syntax (CMS/S-MIME) data, so the CMS routines are not called.
statements.action_statement: Retire this statement once Google Distroless publishes a nodejs24-debian12 rebuild containing libssl3 3.0.20-1~deb12u2 or later, then re-pin the base image digest in Dockerfile and delete this file.

vulnerability.name: CVE-2026-45447
vulnerability.description: openssl: heap use-after-free in PKCS7_verify() (fixed in libssl3 3.0.20-1~deb12u2).

statements.impact_statement: The application does not verify PKCS7/S-MIME structures, so PKCS7_verify is not called.
statements.action_statement: Retire this statement once Google Distroless publishes a nodejs24-debian12 rebuild containing libssl3 3.0.20-1~deb12u2 or later, then re-pin the base image digest in Dockerfile and delete this file.
